steps.title: Identify the signal
steps.content: The publications are frequently cited, and the dash-clarification confirms they're valuable to other scholars. The blank should express a positive relationship between citation frequency and research value.

steps.title: Match the verb meaning
steps.content:
The blank needs to mean "emphasizes or confirms" — frequent citations demonstrate how useful Hall's research is for peers. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — belies: contradicts or disguises — this would mean citations hide her usefulness, the opposite of what the passage says. ✗
  • Choice B — underscores: highlights or reinforces — frequent citations by other scholars directly reinforce how valuable her research is. ✓
  • Choice C — forestalls: prevents or delays — citations don't block anything; they reflect uptake. ✗
  • Choice D — overshadows: diminishes by comparison — this implies citations make her usefulness seem smaller, contradicting the passage's positive framing. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
